channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: Jansen set for debut as Ireland make nine changes for Japan clash
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Sean Jansen will win his first cap in a much-changed Ireland side for Saturday's Nations Championship clash with Japan in Australia.

item.title: Meet your World Cup quarterfinalists! How they got...
item.dc:creator: ESPN Soccer
item.description: And then there were eight. Four weeks after the World Cup began, the quarterfinals start on Thursday with each team three wins away from lifting the trophy.

item.dc:creator: ESPN Soccer
item.description: Individual superstars become even more influential at this point in the tournament.

item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets

item.title: “When I Was Sports Minister, Lawmakers Demanded N200m Bribe During My Budget Defence” – Solomon Dalung
item.dc:creator: Information Nigeria
item.description: Former Minister of Youth and Sports Development, Solomon Dalung, has alleged that members of the National Assembly demanded a N200 million bribe from him during his first budget defence while serving under the administration of former President Muhammadu Buhari. Dalung made the allegation in a state

item.title: ExxonMobil to invest $1 billion in Nigeria’s offshore, targets 40,000 bpd increase
item.dc:creator: Nairametrics
item.description: ExxonMobil and its partners have committed $1 billion to the Usan Infill Project in Nigeria's offshore oil sector, a move expected to increase the country's crude oil production by about 40,000 barrels per day (bpd). The post ExxonMobil to invest $1 billion in Nigeria’s offshore, targets 40,00

item.title: Authenticity, faith, and consistency remain powerful ingredients for lasting success in the digital age — Oshogwe
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: TikTok creator Oshogwe Anthonia Emike shares how authenticity, faith and consistency have helped her build a thriving digital brand. The post Authenticity, faith, and consistency remain powerful ingredients for lasting success in the digital age — Oshogwe appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: Over 8.8million children live with sickle cell disease in Sub-Saharan Africa —Lancet
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: A new large-scale systematic review and modelling analysis has estimated that more than 8.8 million children and adolescents in sub-Saharan Africa were living with sickle cell disease (SCD) in 2023, highlighting a significant but largely preventable public health burden across the region. The study,

item.title: ‘We work for Abuja Alhaji’ – Notorious bandit captured in Delta forest confesses [VIDEO]
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The alleged bandit kingpin arrested in a forest in Delta State, Abubakar Usman, has claimed that he and other members of his gang rear cattle for an Alhaji based in Abuja. DAILY POST recalls that operatives of the Delta State Police Command’s Anti-Kidnapping Squad, in collaboration with the Sa

item.title: Platner's disastrous candidacy exposes rifts that could dampen Democrats' Senate hopes
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: Along with creating tumult in a must-win race for Democrats, Graham Platner's exit is laying bare division between the party's left wing and moderates.

item.title: Suspected ISWAP Terrorists, Families Surrender As Military Rescues Abductees In North East
item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: Headquarters Operation HADIN KAI assured the public that ongoing search and rescue operations will continue with unwavering resolve until every abducted person is accounted for and safely reunited with their families. item.title: ‘Don’t Blame Us Later’: NDC cries out over Peter Obi’s safety
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: “It is even looking like the establishment is actually not interested in his security. They have systematically ensured that he does not get adequate security protection,” Abdulsalam said.

item.title: Idolises Ronaldo, plays like Saka - meet £40m Chelsea winger Quenda
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: Chelsea's new signing Geovany Quenda hopes to follow in the footsteps of Cristiano Ronaldo by leaving Sporting's academy for the challenge and exposure of the Premier League.

item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: Over the past three years, the 360-member House pursued ambitious constitutional reforms, processed major economic legislation and expanded oversight activities. The post Three years of Reps under Tajudeen: Achievements, controversies, scandals appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria.

item.title: Abiodun hails SWDC DG, Charles Akinola at 70, salutes distinguished public service

item.title: 2026 World Cup: Super Eagles icon breaks silence over Folarin Balogun red card debacle

item.description: The 2026 World Cup has been the major topic in the football world since it kicked off in June, Soccernet.ng reports. ​The tournament began last month, and fans from all over the world have been treated to premium football excitement. ​Before the start of the tournament, there was a lot of talk about

item.title: US launches strikes on Iran, Iran targets Kuwait, Bahrain
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: Mr Trump said the US may “de-nuclearise” Iran without a deal. The post US launches strikes on Iran, Iran targets Kuwait, Bahrain appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria.

item.title: ADC bars state chapters from handling court cases
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The African Democratic Congress (ADC) has directed all its state chapters to stop receiving court documents or hiring lawyers on behalf of the party without approval from its national leadership. The directive, issued by the party’s National Publicity Secretary, Bolaji Abdullahi, requires ever

item.title: Nigeria’s vaccine divide leaves the poorest children most exposed to deadly diphtheria
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Nigeria is facing a dangerous gap in childhood protection against diphtheria, with new research showing that children in poorer states are far less likely to be fully vaccinated, leaving many at risk of a disease that can kill within days if untreated. A nationwide study published in Public Health C

item.title: WFP’s report of looming severe hunger in northern Nigeria raises concern
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The United Nations agency, the World Food Programme, WFP, last week raised the alarm over an imminent food crisis in northern Nigeria if the government does not make a concerted effort to control the situation. It warned that the escalating conflict and dwindling humanitarian funding were pushing no

item.title: $133.7M raised so far
item.dc:creator: Technext

item.title: DSS defends detention of journalist over drone probe as Sowore cries foul
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: The DSS said its actions were guided by regulations issued by the Office of the National Security Adviser, which empower it to seize drones and sanction individuals or organisations found operating them without the necessary authorisation.

item.title: World Cup 2026: ‘Key figures’ – Lamine Yamal sends message to Ronaldo, Neymar
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Spain winger, Lamine Yamal, has insisted that Portugal captain, Cristiano Ronaldo and Brazil forward, Neymar are key figures for his generation. Yamal said this while wishing both Ronaldo and Neymar the best in their careers after the duo was recently eliminated from the World Cup. The Barcelona sta

item.title: Dangote effects another cut in petrol price
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: •As diesel prices rises across major depots By Udeme Akpan The Dangote Petroleum Refinery on Wednesday trimmed its ex-depot price of Premium Motor Spirit (PMS), commonly known as petrol, by ¦ 1 per litre, even as diesel prices climbed across several petroleum depots in Lagos, reflecting mixed trends

item.title: 2026 World Cup: SuperComputer predicts teams to qualify from quarter-finals
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: As the 2026 World Cup enters its quarter-final stage, the Opta SuperComputer has given its prediction for teams that will qualify for the last four. France will play Morocco, Spain face Belgium, Norway take on England and Argentina will clash with Switzerland. Opta has given France the highest chanc

item.title: ADC, Atiku, MBF fault Tinubu over ICPC probe of PFIPC
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The African Democratic Congress (ADC), its presidential candidate in the 2027 election, Atiku Abubakar, and the Middle Belt Forum (MBF) have faulted the decision of President Bola Tinubu to hand over the investigation of the ghost agency, the Presidential Foreign Investment Promotion Council (PFIPC)

item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Deputy Speaker of the House of Representatives, Benjamin Kalu, has thrown his weight behind the investigation into the alleged Presidential Foreign Intervention Promotion Council (PFIPC) scandal, revealing that his office was among those misled by documents that appeared to have been issued by the P

item.title: CBN: Standard N100 note remains legal tender
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Emma Ujah, Abuja Bureau Chief The Central Bank of Nigeria (CBN) has stated that the Standard N100 note is still a legal tender and must be accepted for all transactions. The apex bank, in a statement by its Ag. Director, Corporate Communications, Mrs. Hakama Sidi-Ali, yesterday, said the clarific

item.title: World Cup 2026: Brazil’s Filipe Luis snubs France, names country to win trophy
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Former Brazil defender, Filipe Luis, has snubbed France and named Spain to win the FIFA 2026 World Cup. Both Spain and France are in the quarter-final stage at the World Cup. They will face Belgium and Morocco, respectively, in the last-8 fixtures on Thursday and Friday. When asked ab

item.title: War: ‘You will be struck’ – Iran warns US
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Iran’s parliament speaker, Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf, has warned the United States that bullying and breaking promises are no longer without cost. Ghalibaf issued the warning in a post on social media, cautioning against US pressure over the Strait of Hormuz. “Let me be clear; strike, and

item.dc:creator: ESPN Soccer
item.description: The Egyptian FA said it "cannot remain silent regarding the refereeing decisions" following the team's round-of-16 loss to Argentina at the World Cup.

item.title: Mexico start Márquez era on 'strong foundation'
item.dc:creator: ESPN Soccer
item.description: The Mexican Football Federation announced Wednesday that Rafael Márquez is taking the reins as Javier Aguirre's successor to lead the team up to the 2030 World Cup.

item.title: EU lawmakers eye investigating FIFA's Infantino
item.dc:creator: ESPN Soccer
item.description: Dozens of European lawmakers are gathering support to launch an investigation in the European Parliament of FIFA boss Gianni Infantino over his involvement in the decision to permit U.S. striker Folarin Balogun to play despite an earlier red card.

item.dc:creator: ESPN Soccer
item.description: We're into the quarterfinals at the 2026 FIFA World Cup, and the race for the Golden Boot -- given to the tournament's top goal scorer -- is red-hot.

This is despite the recent statement by the party in the State affirming Chris Nkwonta as the

item.title: COFAS calls for Cooperative Development Fund in Anambra

item.description: Laments poor financing, weak governance in the sector By Cynthia Alo The Cooperative Federation of Anambra State Limited, COFAS, has called on the State Government to establish a Cooperative Development Fund, CDF, and integrate cooperatives into the state’s economic planning. COFAS also disclosed th

item.title: Count Bezukhov looks to strike again on St Leger trials day

item.description: Count Bezukhov looks the one to beat in our feature contest from Doncaster today - live on Sky Sports Racing.

item.title: Ethiopia: 10 things to know about TPLF’s Fetlework Gebregziabher
item.dc:creator: The Africa Report
item.description: She built her name in the bush. Ethiopian Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed appointed her a minister, then took the post away from her, and now Fetlework Gebregziabher, aka Monjorino, stands as the loudest voice accusing Addis Ababa of moving troops toward eastern Tigray.

item.title: Transfer: Ederson’s move to Man Utd in danger of collapse
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Ederson’s move from Atalanta to Manchester United this summer could yet fall through. This is because United were not convinced with the results of Ederson’s medicals, after they agreed a €45m deal. The Brazilian midfielder is now set to undergo further checks before the deal is finalize

item.title: Malaysia Says It Still Has to Pay $2 Billion in 1MDB Debts
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: The Malaysian government must pay 8.9 billion ringgit ($2.18 billion) in remaining debts it incurred from the 1Malaysia Development Berhad scandal, Deputy Finance Minister Liew Chin Tong said on Thursday.

item.title: Airtel Africa hits N5,801 as NGX surges N3.45 trillion; YTD return reclaims 55% threshold
item.dc:creator: Nairametrics
item.description: The Nigerian equities market delivered its most powerful single-session gain in weeks on Wednesday, July 8, 2026, as Airtel Africa's 10% maximum daily advance to N5,801.40 anchored a broad-based rally that added N3.45 trillion to investors’ wealth. item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: “So, maybe Obi needs a psychiatric evaluation to know whether or not he is hallucinating. As far as we are concerned, the statement he made was born out of hallucination,” he said.

item.description: What a World Cup it has been with great goals, thrilling comebacks, late drama, shock results. But how will this tournament be remembered?

item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: Over 70 European lawmakers have called for an investigation into FIFA and its president Gianni Infantino regarding the principle of political neutrality, talkSPORT reports. Infantino has faced huge backlash in recent days, following the decision to suspend Folarin Balogun’s red card ban ahead

item.title: El-Rufai’s wife raises concerns over bail conditions
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Asiya El-Rufai, wife of former Kaduna State Governor Nasir El-Rufai, has expressed concern over the bail conditions granted to her husband, describing them as difficult to meet. The post El-Rufai’s wife raises concerns over bail conditions appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: Favourite child syndrome: The influence of parental preference on their children’s mental health
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Bola is the middle of five children. Her older sister was “the pretty one”. Her younger sister was “the baby”. Her two brothers were automatically valuable as the boys. She was nothing, not the first, last, a male or particularly beautiful by her family’s standards. She describes her chi

Iheanacho joined the Hoops on a free transfer last summer after leaving Spanish club Sevilla. The 29-year-old signed a one-year contract with Celtic, w

The bill, sponsored by Eli Ankala (APC, Rukuba-Irigwe), was debated during Tuesday’s plena

The state chapters have also been asked to always refer any court processes s

item.description: Recently, two weeks ago to be precise, I found myself in an ambush, facing something akin to a Spanish Inquisition. I had attended the wedding of a friend’s daughter in London. item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: Former Governor of Sokoto State, Senator Aminu Waziri Tambuwal, has said only courageous politicians who are committed to serving the people rather than pursuing personal interests can join the African Democratic Congress (ADC). Tambuwal made the remark on Wednesday while receiving the immediate pas

item.description: Seven people have been killed and seven others, including three university students, abducted in separate attacks in Sokoto, Bauchi and Nasarawa states. In Sokoto State, bandits attacked Lajinge community in Sabon Birni Local Government Area in the early hours of Wednesday, killing six residents and

The association, in a report released by its Director General, Seg

item.title: Common scent leaf may help improve male fertility —Experts
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: A common Nigerian cooking herb known as scent leaf may hold promise for improving male fertility by supporting both reproductive health and the balance of beneficial bacteria in the gut, according to a new scientific review. The review, published in the Journal of Complementary and Alternative Medic

item.title: Pakistan Seeks Urgent LNG Cargo as Hormuz Attacks Disrupt Supply
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Pakistan is urgently seeking to purchase a shipment of liquefied natural gas after fresh hostilities in the Strait of Hormuz again disrupted supplies.

item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: According to Obasa, kidnapping, banditry and other violent crimes cannot be effectively tackled without allowing states greater control over policing.

item.title: Trump notifies Congress of plan to delist Syria as terror sponsor
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Removing Syria from the State Sponsors of Terrorism list is a separate legal process. The designation has long restricted US foreign assistance, defence exports, financial transactions and other forms of engagement with Syria.

item.title: Tata Group Targets $100 Billion Revenue from Auto Units by 2031
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Tata Group is targeting about $100 billion in automotive revenue by the year ended March 2031, Chairman Natarajan Chandrasekaran said, outlining growth plans across its four automotive businesses following the demerger of Tata Motors’ passenger and commercial vehicle operations.

item.description: Title favourites France face African champions Morocco, as football's 2026 World Cup enters the last-eight phase.

item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: Trump says the attacks are "retribution for yesterday's bombing of ships by Iran". Iran earlier promised strong retaliation.

item.description: The son of Mexican man, Lorenzo Salgado Araujo, who was fatally shot by an officer from ICE.

item.title: Lagos rescues unconscious pregnant woman, hands mother, baby to Plateau govt
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Chioma Obinna A 22-year-old pregnant woman found unconscious on a Lagos street has been rescued from the brink of death, delivered of a healthy baby girl and reunited with the Plateau State Government in what officials described as another success story of the Lagos State emergency healthcare int

item.title: Supreme Court goes fully digital, ditches paper records to curb tampering
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Ikechukwu Nnochiri On April 23, Vanguard Law & Human Rights highlighted some of the challenges plaguing the Nigerian judiciary, chief among them the judiciary’s continued reliance on analogue systems. This factor, it noted, was partly responsible for the slow pace of justice administration in

item.title: Unjustified demurrages: How NSC saved shippers N86.06bn in three years
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Since his appointment in November 2023, the Executive Secretary/CEO of the Nigerian Shippers Council, Dr. Pius Akutah, has waded through halting unjustified demurrage payment to securing statutory funding for the Council, amongst other achievements. In this report, TOLA ADENUBI looks at the Council

item.title: Spider venom kills varroa mites without harming honeybees
item.dc:creator: Hacker News
item.description: Article URL: https://connectsci.au/news/news-parent/9703/Spider-venom-kills-varroa-mites-without-harming Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48841259 Points: 48 # Comments: 13

item.title: World Cup 2026: Norway Must Watch Out For Rice –Odegaard Warns
item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: Norway star Martin Odegaard has warned his teammates to keep an eye on England midfielder Declan Rice ahead of Saturday’s quarter-final showdown in the ongoing FIFA World Cup 2026. The two Arsenal stars will temporarily set aside their club camaraderie as Norway seeks to achieve another upset

item.title: Australia Agrees to Sell Uranium to India During Modi Visit
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Australia has agreed to sell uranium to India for power generation in a landmark deal signed between the two nations during a visit to Melbourne by Prime Minister Narendra Modi.

item.title: NDDC pushes for food security, job creation in upcoming summit
item.dc:creator: BusinessDay
item.description: The Niger Delta Development Commission (NDDC) is set to mount a world class agric investment summit. The upcoming summit is read more NDDC pushes for food security, job creation in upcoming summit

item.title: Court Grants Tiktoker, Peller Bail In Case Involving Lagos State Police
item.dc:creator: Information Nigeria
item.description: A Lagos State Magistrates’ Court has granted TikTok streamer Habeeb “Peller” Hamzat bail for ₦500,000 in a case involving the Lagos State Police Command. The Police Public Relations Officer, Abimbola Adebisi, confirmed that the content creator was arrested and subsequently arraigned before the court

item.title: Nigeria: Renaissance aims to develop former Shell offshore field first drilled in 1967
item.dc:creator: The Africa Report
item.description: Renaissance Africa Energy has moved to develop the JK field in the Niger Delta, a former Shell offshore asset first drilled in 1967 but left dormant for nearly six decades after announcing a significant oil discovery that could accelerate its commercialisation.

item.title: Joint Security Team Captures Wanted Bandit And Rescues Kidnapped Woman
item.dc:creator: SDK Celeb
item.description: A joint security team in Delta State has arrested one of the most wanted bandits in the region and safely rescued a woman kidnapped on July 2, 2026 .Human rights activist and security volunteer Harrison Gwamnishu confirmed that Mrs. Blessing Chiedu from Umunede was abducted along the Ani‑Ifekide Far
